benefits.title: Course teams can claim their graduate futures value with confidence
benefits.statement: Course teams receive trusted course-level evidence and complete a structured review that enables them to understand and articulate the graduate futures value of their course with confidence.
benefits.beneficiary: Course teams, departments, faculties, students, applicants, recruitment teams and King's Edge
benefits.owner: Locally designated curriculum owner, according to faculty arrangements
benefits.successLooksLike: Each in-scope course has an approved graduate futures pack, completes a structured review and produces a course-confirmed Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page that is evidence-linked, appropriately caveated and suitable for agreed use.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack
Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page template

enabledBy.note: The first rollout produces course-owned claims and actions.
The second rollout completes coverage across the remaining in-scope courses.

benefits.title: Course teams can take evidence-informed action on graduate futures
benefits.statement: Course and department teams use graduate futures evidence and structured sense-making to identify proportionate actions, assign ownership and progress them through normal curriculum, planning or enhancement routes, with the aim of strengthening how courses support students' graduate futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Course teams, departments, faculties and students
benefits.owner: Locally designated curriculum owner, with faculty education leadership and existing quality-enhancement oversight
benefits.successLooksLike: Where a review identifies an opportunity or need, the course records a proportionate action, names an owner and incorporates it into the appropriate existing quality enhancement route. Supported conclusions that no additional action is required are also valid.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Graduate Futures Claim and Action on a Page template

enabledBy.note: The first rollout records course-owned conclusions and actions.
The second rollout extends the model across the remaining in-scope courses.
